name: dsp-pandas

metadata.name: dsp-pandas
metadata.summary: Pandas related custom functions for data analysis.

documentation:
# Custom Pandas functions

`dsp_pandas` is a Python package to gather pandas related custom functions. It's only
dependencies should be pandas - and pandas internal dependencies.

modules.name: dsp_pandas.io
documentation: Helper to read and write data from and to different formats.

functions.name: dsp_pandas.io.read_all_excel_sheets
documentation:
Import all sheets as pandas DataFrames from an Excel table into a SimpleNamespace object.

Parameters
----------
fname : str | Path
    Path to the Excel file.
index_col : int, optional
    Index columns to use, by default 0
drop_empty : bool, optional
    Wheater to drop the entirely missing columns although they might be named, by default True

Returns
-------
SimpleNamespace
    Object with all the sheet names as attributes. White space are replaced by underscores.

modules.name: dsp_pandas.format
documentation: Format pandas display options for better readability.

functions.name: dsp_pandas.format.set_pandas_options
documentation: Update default pandas options for better display.

functions.name: dsp_pandas.format.set_pandas_number_formatting
documentation: Format large numbers with commas and decimals.

modules.name: dsp_pandas
documentation:
Group of functions to work with pandas DataFrames.
Can be move to a separate package so it can be used in all projects.

Currently mainly taken from the following sources on PyPI:
- pimms-learn
- njab

modules.name: dsp_pandas.df
documentation: Helpers to identify interesting data in a pandas DataFrame.

functions.name: dsp_pandas.df.unique_cols
documentation:
Check all entries are equal in pandas.Series

Ref: https://stackoverflow.com/a/54405767/968487

Parameters
----------
s : pandas.Series
    Series to check uniqueness

Returns
-------
bool
    Boolean on if all values are equal.

functions.name: dsp_pandas.df.get_unique_and_non_unique_columns
documentation:
Get back a namespace with an column.Index both
of the unique and non-unique columns.

Parameters
----------
df : pandas.DataFrame
    DataFrame to check for unique columns.

Returns
-------
types.SimpleNamespace
    SimpleNamespace with `unique` and `non_unique` column names indices.

functions.name: dsp_pandas.df.drop_unique_columns
documentation: Filter out non-unique columns from a DataFrame.

functions.name: dsp_pandas.df.drop_non_unique_columns
documentation: Filter out non-unique columns from a DataFrame.

functions.name: dsp_pandas.df.combine_value_counts
documentation:
Pass a selection of columns to combine it's value counts.

This performs no checks. Make sure the scale of the variables
you pass is comparable.

Parameters
----------
X : pandas.DataFrame
    A DataFrame of several columns with values in a similar range.
dropna : bool, optional
    Exclude NA values from counting, by default True

Returns
-------
pandas.DataFrame
    DataFrame of combined value counts.

modules.name: dsp_pandas.df.missing_data
documentation:
Missing value related functions for pandas DataFrames.

Taken from pimms-learn.

functions.name: dsp_pandas.df.missing_data.percent_missing
documentation:
Total percentage of missing values in a DataFrame.

Parameters
----------
df : pd.DataFrame
    DataFrame with data.

Returns
-------
float
    Proportion of missing values in the DataFrame.

functions.name: dsp_pandas.df.missing_data.decompose_NAs
documentation:
Decompose missing values by a level into real and indirectly imputed missing values
as defined by the index level.

Real missing value have missing for all samples in a group. Indirectly imputed missing
values are imputed by the the observed
values in that group, e.g. the mean (or median) of it's measurements.

Parameters
----------
data : pd.DataFrame
    DataFrame with samples in columns and features in rows.
level : Union[int, str]
    Index level to group by. Examples: Protein groups, peptides or precursors in MS data.
label : int, optional
    Column name of single column dataframe returned, by default 'summary'

Returns
-------
pd.DataFrame
    One column DataFrame with summary information about missing values.
